articles.title: Japanese Yen steadies as robust Japan growth, Fed hawkish risks offset
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 159.05 at the time of writing on Wednesday, virtually unchanged on the day, as investors balance several opposing factors surrounding the Japanese Yen (JPY) and the US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: US Dollar Index Price Forecast: Journey towards 100.00 remains intact amid hawkish Fed bets
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback’s value against six major currencies, reflects strength ahead of the United States (US) stock markets' opening near its six-week high of 99.40.

articles.title: US Dollar: Fed minutes to clarify hawkish momentum – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Global Strategy Team expects the April FOMC minutes to clarify how many participants backed removing the easing bias and how divided the committee was after several hawkish dissents.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Needs to sustain above 50% Fibo retracement to extend rally
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair is up 0.2% to near 1.3772 during the European trading session on Wednesday. The Loonie pair reflects strength as the Canadian Dollar (CAD) underperforms its peers due to easing expectations that the Bank of Canada (BoC) will raise interest rates in the July policy meeting.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Softer CPI trims BoC hike odds – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank highlights that weaker‑than‑expected Canadian CPI reduced the perceived need for near‑term Bank of Canada tightening.

articles.title: US Dollar: Yield momentum supported by FOMC signals – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Derek Halpenny argues that US Dollar strength is underpinned by rising US yields and a more hawkish shift from the Federal Reserve. He expects the FOMC minutes to reinforce inflation concerns and limit dovish expectations.

articles.title: United Kingdom: Softer inflation but policy risks – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank's Head of Macro Strategy Elwin de Groot highlights that United Kingdom (UK) Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation for April undershot consensus, with services inflation easing, while UK Producer Price Index (PPI) surprised to the upside.

articles.title: Eurozone Inflation Accelerates to 3.0% as Energy Costs Push Headline Higher
articles.expand: Eurozone inflation accelerated in April as rising energy prices pushed headline CPI higher, though underlying core inflation pressures eased modestly. Final data showed Eurozone CPI rose from 2.6% yoy to 3.0% yoy. Core CPI, excluding energy, food, alcohol and tobacco, slowed slightly from 2.3% yoy to 2.2% yoy. The rebound in headline inflation was ...

articles.title: Australian Dollar recovers slightly; firm USD to cap the upside ahead of FOMC Minutes
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair attracts some buyers following an intraday dip to the 0.7085 region on Wednesday and moves away from its lowest level since April 14, touched the previous day.

articles.title: Euro area headline inflation confirmed to pick up further in April amid Middle East war
articles.expand: CPI +3.0% vs +3.0% y/y prelim Prior +2.6% Core CPI +2.2% vs +2.2% y/y prelim Prior +2.3% Services inflation continues to be the main sticking point, but is at least seen easing to 3.0% in April (previously 3.3%). Meanwhile, food price inflation is still keeping thereabouts at 2.4% (previously 2.5%). But with regards to the headline reading, energy ...

articles.title: ECB’s Wunsch: The ECB will have to react at some point
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker and the head of Belgium's central bank, Pierre Wunsch, said during the European trading session on Wednesday that the central bank needs to act at some point, as “we are at the beginning of an inflation problem”.

articles.title: Brent: Oil holds above 110 as Strait risk persists – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank analysts report Brent Oil trading above $110 per barrel as the Strait of Hormuz remains constrained, sustaining a tight link between Oil and global yields.

articles.title: British Pound flatlines against the Japanese Yen, amid soft UK CPI data
articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) is trading practically flat against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Wednesday, moving halfway through the last three weeks’ trading range, a few pips below 213.00.

articles.title: ECB policymaker Wunsch warns we are at the beginning of an inflation problem
articles.expand: We are at the beginning of an inflation problem The ECB will have to react at some point ECB's Wunsch warns that we are at the beginning of an inflation problem and the prolonged geopolitical crisis raises the risk that higher costs will feed into inflation expectations and core inflation. Wunsch has been in the hawkish camp for a while and have be...

articles.title: Gold bounces off late March lows ahead of FOMC Minutes; not out of the woods yet
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) recovers slightly from its lowest level since March 30, touched early this Wednesday, though it remains on the back foot below the $4,500 psychological mark through the first half of the European session. Investors remain skeptical about a potential US-Iran peace deal.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls await 200-EMA breakout near 1.3770 ahead of FOMC Minutes
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts some dip-buyers on Wednesday and climbs to the 1.3765-1.3770 region during the first half of the European session, back closer to its highest level since April 14, touched the previous day.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Dollar strength dominates as US yields rise – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Derek Halpenny highlights growing downside risks for the Japanese Yen as rising US yields and higher Fed hike probabilities support the Dollar.

articles.title: ECB policymaker nominee Moulin: Too soon to say if ECB needs to act in June
articles.expand: European Central Bank policymaker and Bank of France nominee Emmanuel Moulin said during the European trading session on Wednesday that it is too soon to say whether the central bank will make any monetary policy adjustment in the June policy meeting.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: Inflation cools BoC expectations – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister notes that Canadian inflation surprised to downside on core measures, staying near 2%, helped by Canada’s status as a net energy exporter and a weak labour market. He questions market expectations for two Bank of Canada (BoC) hikes by year-end.

articles.title: Soon to be ECB policymaker Moulin says it's too soon to say if ECB needs to act in June
articles.expand: Too soon to say if ECB needs to act in June Need to pay close attention to inflation The Bank of France nominee Emmanuel Moulin, who is going to replace Francois Villeroy as the next ECB governor, said that it's still too early to commit to any specific action at the upcoming June meeting. articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the main highlight was the UK CPI report. articles.expand: UK inflation slowed more sharply than expected in April, offering the Bank of England some near-term relief as both headline and core price pressures cooled to multi-year lows. articles.title: Asia open: Surging 30-year bond yield flirts with 5.20% as market eyes Nvidia and Bank of England dilemma
articles.expand: Global markets remained under pressure as surging bond yields pushed the US 30-year Treasury yield toward the key 5.20% level, its highest since 2007. Rising inflation fears and growing expectations of future Federal Reserve rate hikes weighed heavily on technology stocks ahead of Nvidia’s highly anticipated earnings release.

articles.title: UK Jobs Market Takes a Hit as Iran War Energy Shock Bites
articles.expand: UK payrolled employees fell 100,000 in April — the biggest monthly drop since COVID — as the Iran war energy shock hammers businesses and sends the unemployment rate rising.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Correction has further legs to go
